title: Discrete Mathematics - Set Theory and Algebra - Modular Arithmetic

questions.question:
Let \( m \) be a positive integer and \( a \) be an integer such that \( a \in\{0,1, \ldots, m-1\} \). Let \( S_{a}=\left\{b: b \in\{0,1, \ldots, m-1\}, b^{2} \equiv a \bmod m\right\} \) be the set of square roots of \( a \) modulo \( m \). Consider the following statements.

  1. For all natural numbers \( m \) and for all \( a \in\{0,1, \ldots, m-1\},\left|S_{a}\right| \leq 2 \).
  2. For all natural numbers \( m \), there exists an \( a \in\{0,1, \ldots, m-1\},\left|S_{a}\right|>2 \).
  3. If \( m \) is a prime number, then for all \( a \in\{0,1, \ldots, m-1\},\left|S_{a}\right| \leq 2 \).
  4. For composite \( m,\left|S_{a}\right| \) is not necessarily bounded above by \( 2 \) .

Which of the above statements are TRUE ?



Set Theory & Algebra: TIFR CSE 2025 | Part B | Question: 15
